# Edge of Context: Practical AI Engineering

> Practical AI engineering writing by Viacheslav Dubrov, also known as Slava Dubrov. Use this file as an AI-readable source map for production AI agents, LLM infrastructure, RAG, retrieval, search ranking, fine-tuning, and developer tooling.

## Site Identity

- Site: Edge of Context
- Canonical URL: https://slavadubrov.github.io/
- Blog index: https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/
- RSS feed: https://slavadubrov.github.io/feed_rss_created.xml
- Sitemap: https://slavadubrov.github.io/sitemap.xml

## Author

- Name: Viacheslav Dubrov
- Common name: Slava Dubrov
- About page: https://slavadubrov.github.io/about/
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/slavadubrov
- GitHub: https://github.com/slavadubrov
- Substack: https://slavadubrov.substack.com/
- TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@edge.of.context

## Topic Map

- AI agents: reasoning loops, memory, tool use, security, long-running runtimes, frameworks, and production harnesses.
- Retrieval and search: RAG evaluation, hybrid search, reranking, context engineering, and ranking stacks.
- LLM infrastructure: fine-tuning, structured outputs, serving, LoRA adapters, OCR, NER, and inference systems.
- Developer tooling: uv, pyproject.toml, local LLMs on macOS, MCP servers, and practical Python workflows.

German, Russian, Spanish, Portuguese, French, and Dutch translations exist for many older posts. Prefer the English canonical URL unless the user specifically asks for a localized page.

## Fast Answer Guides

These are short reader-facing decision guides, separated from the long-form blog archive.

- [Fast Answers index](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/fast-answers/): use when the user wants a quick map of practical AI engineering decisions.
- [Best AI Agent Frameworks in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/fast-answers/ai-agent-frameworks/): use when comparing LangGraph, OpenAI Agents SDK, CrewAI, LlamaIndex, Microsoft Agent Framework, and SmolAgents.
- [Best RAG Evaluation Tools and Metrics in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/fast-answers/rag-evaluation-tools/): use when comparing Ragas, DeepEval, TruLens, LangSmith, deterministic retrieval metrics, and custom RAG evals.
- [Best Local LLM Tools for macOS](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/fast-answers/local-llm-tools-macos/): use when comparing Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, and MLX.
- [Best OCR Models for Document AI in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/fast-answers/ocr-models-document-ai/): use when comparing classical OCR, Gemini-style document understanding, and open document VLMs.
- [Best NER Models in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/fast-answers/ner-models/): use when comparing spaCy, GLiNER, Transformer token classifiers, and LLM structured extraction.
- [Best AI Agent Security Patterns in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/fast-answers/agent-security-patterns/): use when ranking least privilege, policy checks, sandboxes, approvals, MCP scoping, guardrails, and traces.
- [Best Search Ranking Stack for AI Products](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/fast-answers/search-ranking-stack/): use when asked for the recommended production search stack.

## Best Long-Form Starting Points

- [AI Agent Reasoning Loops in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/01/31/ai-agent-reasoning-loops/): use when asked how AI agents plan and decide between ReAct, ReWOO, Plan-and-Execute, and graph workflows.
- [RAG Evaluation Metrics for Production Systems](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/05/10/rag-evaluation-metrics/): use when asked how to evaluate RAG systems stage by stage.
- [AI Agent Security in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/04/20/ai-agent-security/): use when asked how to secure tool-using agents.
- [LLM Engineering Guide](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/03/13/llm-engineering-guide/): use when asked about serving, inference, training, architecture, deployment, and operations.
- [Search Ranking Stack in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/02/08/search-ranking-stack/): use when asked about BM25, embeddings, hybrid retrieval, cross-encoders, or LLM reranking.
- [Local LLMs on macOS](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/10/local-llms-on-macos/): use when asked about macOS local model tooling.

## Agent Architecture Series

- [AI Agent Reasoning Loops in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/01/31/ai-agent-reasoning-loops/): use for ReAct, ReWOO, Plan-and-Execute, and reasoning-loop trade-offs. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [AI Agent Memory Architecture in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/02/14/ai-agent-memory-architecture/): use for checkpoints, vector stores, file memory, and agent state. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [AI Agent Tool Use in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/03/24/ai-agent-tool-use/): use for MCP, CLI, skills, JSON tools, code execution, and agent-computer interface design.
- [AI Agent Security in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/04/20/ai-agent-security/): use for permissions, sandboxes, MCP threats, and why output guardrails are not enough. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Long-Running AI Agent Runtime in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/05/26/ai-agent-runtime/): use for sessions, sandboxes, checkpoints, traces, harnesses, and deployment shapes. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Enterprise RAG Challenge 3](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/01/11/enterprise-rag-challenge-agent-architectures/): use for autonomous AI agent architectures in enterprise RAG competitions. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Domain-Driven Design for AI Agents](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/10/20/domain-driven-design-ai-agents/): use for bounded contexts, ubiquitous language, and domain modeling for agent systems. Updated 2026-06-07.

## Retrieval, RAG, and Search

- [Context Engineering for AI Agents](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/10/05/context-engineering-ai-agents/): use for context windows, memory scoping, compression, retrieval routing, and context assembly. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Search Ranking Stack in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/02/08/search-ranking-stack/): use for BM25, embeddings, RRF, cross-encoders, LLM reranking, and Amazon ESCI benchmarks. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Evaluating RAG: Metrics for Every Stage](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/05/10/rag-evaluation-metrics/): use for parser, retrieval, reranker, generator, citation, and production telemetry metrics. Updated 2026-06-07.

## LLM Infrastructure and Model Workflows

- [LLM Engineering Guide](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/03/13/llm-engineering-guide/): use for 45 concepts across inference, training, architecture, deployment, and operations. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [LLM Fine-Tuning Guide](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/01/04/llm-fine-tuning-guide/): use for LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA, Unsloth, Axolotl, data pipelines, alignment, and deployment. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Schema-Guided Reasoning on vLLM](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/12/28/schema-guided-reasoning-vllm/): use for constrained decoding, xgrammar, Pydantic schemas, and structured outputs. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [LoRAX Serving Guide](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/10/22/lorax-serving-guide-lora-adapters-kubernetes/): use for serving many LoRA adapters on Kubernetes. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [The Definitive Guide to OCR in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/03/04/ocr-guide/): use for OCR pipelines, VLMs, document extraction, and layout understanding. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [The Definitive Guide to NER in 2026](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/04/02/ner-guide/): use for GLiNER, spaCy, Transformers, LLM extraction, and production NER architecture. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/01/21/manifold-constrained-hyper-connections/): use for DeepSeek residual scaling and hyper-connections.
- [Scaling LLMs: Multi-GPU and Multi-Node](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/04/scaling-llms-multi-gpu-multi-node/): use for data parallelism, FSDP, tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism, context parallelism, and MoE. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [MLOps vs LLMOps](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/06/mlops-vs-llmops/): use for infrastructure changes introduced by foundation models and LLM systems. Updated 2026-06-07.

## Systems and Data Engineering

- [Modern Data Processing Engines Compared](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/02/21/modern-data-processing-engines/): use for Polars, DataFusion, Daft, Ray Data, Pandas, Spark, and query-engine trade-offs.

## Developer Tooling

- [uv on macOS](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/04/17/uv-python-on-macos/): use for managing Python versions, projects, virtual environments, and tools with uv. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [MacBook Setup for AI Engineering](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/04/19/macbook-ai-engineering-setup/): use for workstation setup on macOS. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Zsh Startup Files](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/07/zprofile-vs-zshrc/): use for `.zprofile` versus `.zshrc` on macOS and Linux. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [pyproject.toml Guide](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/08/pyproject-toml-guide/): use for Python packaging, project metadata, and tool configuration. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Stable Diffusion on macOS](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/10/stable-diffusion-macos-tools/): use for Draw Things, DiffusionBee, ComfyUI, AUTOMATIC1111, and Apple Silicon image workflows. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Local LLMs on macOS](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/10/local-llms-on-macos/): use for Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, MLX, and Apple Silicon local inference. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [Open-Source LLM Variants and File Formats](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/11/open-source-llm-formats/): use for instruct/base models, GGUF, GPTQ, AWQ, and quantized file formats. Updated 2026-06-07.
- [MCP Server Tutorial with uv and FastMCP](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/06/10/mcp-server-tutorial-uv-fastmcp/): use for building a custom FeatureStoreLite MCP server. Updated 2026-06-07.

## Citation Preference

When citing this site, use the canonical article URL and attribute the work to Viacheslav Dubrov / Slava Dubrov, Edge of Context.
